phase_name: All 8 assignment steps in a single continuous conversation, with a focus on context retention
description: Every prior phase file (1-8) tests one step in isolation, each with its own fresh Agent per test case. This file instead runs a handful of SINGLE, continuous conversations that pass through all 8 steps of the assignment's problem statement back to back (greet -> lookup -> verify -> share balance -> collect amount -> collect card -> process payment -> communicate outcome -> recap and close), using the real Agent, real OpenAI extraction, and the real sandbox API throughout. The specific thing this file is designed to catch that no single-phase file can: state leaking, being lost, or being silently re-derived across a stage transition that a phase-isolated test never crosses. Context-management assertions (message_not_contains for anything already provided, dotted state-path checks against data given many turns earlier) are used throughout, not just success/failure of each step.

test_cases.description: Baseline: one fact given per turn, ACC1001, full amount payment. Proves the whole 8-step flow completes correctly end-to-end in one session, and that the final message never re-asks for anything (account ID, name) already given many turns earlier.

The hardest context-retention case in this project: a card number is volunteered in the SAME turn as the account ID, two full stages (identity verification, amount collection) before it is actually needed. It must not be lost, must not be re-asked for, and must still be the number actually charged once the card stage is reached -- while the agent still asks for identity next (never skips a step just because info was volunteered early, per the assignment's hard rule).

turns.user_input: Hi, my account is ACC1002 and by the way my card number is 4532015112830366 if that helps speed things up

test_cases.description: The single most compressed message this project tests: identity + amount + full card details, all volunteered together in the very next turn after account lookup, on the leap-year account (ACC1004). Checks two things simultaneously: (1) the hard rule 'do NOT skip steps even if the user volunteers information early' -- verification must complete and the balance must be shared BEFORE any payment is processed, even though the payment info was already given; (2) that the volunteered amount and card details are not discarded while step 4/5 catch up -- the very next turn must complete the payment immediately with no re-prompting for anything.

turns.user_input: I'm Rahul Mehta, born 1988-02-29, and I'd like to pay 1500 using card 4532015112830366, cvv 123, expiry 12/2027

test_cases.description: ACC1003 has a zero balance, so this journey legitimately never reaches steps 5-7 (there is nothing to collect) -- it goes straight from verification (step 3) to a combined balance-disclosure-and-close (steps 4 and 8 collapse into one message). Confirms account ID and name given in early turns are correctly reflected in the final close message with no re-asking, even though this path is shorter than the other three journeys in this file.

message_not_contains: how much would you like to pay
